typical_relief_triggers: Finding safe open areas in maze
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear exploration with sporadic combat encounters and environmental hazards; slower character movement creates deliberate pacing rather than action-driven rhythm

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Secret areas and hidden objects accessible through exploration; invisible items only grabbable at certain moments during jumping
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Extensive hidden areas and invisible items throughout cave maze; exploration rewards discovery of optional content

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic movement, shooting, and climbing mechanics with minimal hazard pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expanded enemy variety, invisible item mechanics, and key-locked door progression; maze navigation becomes more complex
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multiple objectives requiring item collection and rescue operations; must locate Raj diamond and perform return journey
notable_spikes: Introduction of invisible item mechanic requiring precision jumping
Multiple simultaneous rescue objectives with item dependencies
Return journey requiring navigation memory and resource management

notable_level_patterns.description: Large-scale subterranean cave system designed for player agency in route selection; described as 'large scale maze' in contemporary reviews
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Andes Mountains cavern system (main game structure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Climb and swim upward mechanics suggest vertical shaft areas requiring precise directional input and platform navigation
Playing card suit items unlock rock doors, gating progression and encouraging thorough exploration
Rhonda (turned to stone) and Quickclaw (trapped) require specific items to free; creates secondary puzzle layers
